title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: For most of human history, our lives were (0) governed by the natural cycles of light and dark. However, the invention of electric light has (1) transformed our modern environment, allowing us to remain active long after the sun has set. While this has (2) to significant economic and social benefits, it has also created a phenomenon known as light pollution. In many cities, the night sky is so bright that the stars are no longer visible, a loss that scientists believe affects our sense of (3) in the universe. Beyond the aesthetic impact, excessive artificial light can (4) with the biological clocks of both humans and wildlife. For example, nocturnal animals often become disoriented, (5) their ability to hunt or migrate successfully. Researchers are now (6) for the use of 'dark-sky friendly' lighting that directs light downward where it is needed, rather than scattering it into the atmosphere. By making small changes to our urban infrastructure, we can reduce energy waste and (7) our natural heritage. This ongoing effort (8) as a reminder that even the most beneficial technologies can have unintended consequences for the planet if they are not managed with care for the delicate balance of the global environment and our shared future.

example.explanation: “Governed by” is the standard formal way to describe being controlled or influenced by a system or cycle.

questions.explanation: “Profoundly transformed” is a strong C1 collocation for a deep and fundamental change.
“Led to” is the correct phrasal verb meaning to result in a particular outcome.
“Sense of place” is an idiomatic expression referring to a feeling of belonging or location.
“Interfere with” is the standard verb for disrupting a natural process or system.
“Hampering” means to make it difficult for someone to do something or for something to happen.
“Advocating for” is a formal verb meaning to publicly support a particular cause or policy.
“Preserve” is the correct formal verb for protecting something valuable for the future.
“Serves as a reminder” is a fixed formal collocation.
